steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence names two paintings completed in 1570 and a second year (the blank), then states a separate fact about allegorical art’s popularity. Left of the blank is the independent clause “Two of the most celebrated examples… were completed in 1570 and [blank].” Right of the blank is another independent clause: “such allegorical artwork was particularly popular from the 15th through the late 18th centuries.” The blank must (1) supply the missing year 1712, (2) include “respectively” to clarify which painting corresponds to which year, and (3) join the two independent clauses correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1712, respectively;: Provides the year, places “respectively” as a parenthetical after it, and uses a semicolon to correctly join the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — 1712, respectively,: Uses a comma instead of a semicolon, creating a comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — respectively,: Omits the year 1712 entirely, leaving the sentence without the second date both paintings need. ✗
  • Choice D — 1712; respectively,: Places the semicolon between the year and “respectively,” separating “respectively” from the clause it modifies and making “respectively,” a fragment. ✗
A is correct.
